#COMP110 Data Analysis Project

## About Me
Hi! My name is Emmy Vidarte, and I am a student in COMP110. This project explores survey data from students in the course to better understand how different factors impact learning.

## Analysis Summary

In this project, I analyzed survey data from COMP110 students to see if pre-lecture videos improve student understanding.

I focused on the following variables:
- Pre-lecture videos
- Student understanding
- Course difficulty

Using Python, I processed the data and created visualizations to find patterns and relationships between these variables.

## Visualizations

### Pre-Lecture Video Ratings
![Pre Lecture Histogram](static/imgs/pre_hist.png)

### Student Understanding Ratings
![Understanding Histogram](static/imgs/understanding_hist.png)

### Relationship Between Pre-Lecture Videos and Understanding
![Scatter Plot](static/imgs/scatter.png)

## Conclusion

Based on my analysis, there appears to be a slight positive relationship between students who find pre-lecture videos helpful and their level of understanding. This suggests that pre-lecture videos may support student learning, although the relationship is not very strong.

Because the data does not show a perfectly clear trend, the results are somewhat inconclusive. Other factors, such as prior experience or study habits, may also influence understanding.

One potential recommendation is to use more pre-lecture videos, but continue collecting more data to better evaluate their effectiveness.

A possible downside is that creating pre-lecture videos requires more time and effort from instructors. Some students may also choose not to use them, which would reduce their impact.

In the future, the course could improve this idea by collecting more detailed feedback specifically about how students use pre-lecture videos and how often they engage with them.
